config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan dan perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankan individu begitu saja. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika setiap orang merasa aman dan dihormati haknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically come before individual rights, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. Public health, safety, and basic order do matter, but they only justify limits on individual rights when those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If we make "society" the default priority, it becomes too easy to silence minorities or punish unpopular people in the name of the common good. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices the individual first; it is one that protects individual rights while still allowing reasonable collective action.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan kelompok minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak adil, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas kolektif yang terlalu absolut. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk ketertiban dan krisis seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik, tetapi batasnya harus jelas, proporsional, dan tetap menghormati hak dasar. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar kuat justru yang melindungi individunya, bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that framing has repeatedly been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government abuse. That said, I also do not think individual rights are absolute in every situation: things like public health, emergency response, and basic safety can require real limits, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to strong oversight. What I reject is the idea that “the common good” should be the default trump card, because once you do that, unpopular people and minorities are the first ones to lose. A free society should protect individuals first and then build collective solutions around those rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam pengalaman Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi alat untuk membungkam kritik atau menekan kelompok yang lemah, jadi saya tidak percaya pada logika mayoritas sebagai kartu truf. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik di mana pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus ketat, proporsional, dan diawasi. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a United States perspective, I am wary of any argument that gives “the common good” automatic authority, because it has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power, especially against unpopular groups. At the same time, I do think there are narrow cases like public health emergencies or immediate safety threats where limited restrictions are justified, but they should be temporary, evidence-based, and tightly checked. A strong society is not one that can overrule individuals whenever it wants; it is one that protects rights first and uses collective action within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
